title: cyphostemma mandrakense
intro: Cyphostemma mandrakense, also known as the 'Madagascar Resurrection Plant', is a succulent species native to the island of Madagascar. It is known for its ability to revive itself after being submerged in water and then drying out, hence its name 'Resurrection Plant'. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Cyphostemma mandrakense are attributed to its extracts, which can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, making it a valuable plant in the field of ethnobotany.
